OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER FILED BY PLAINTIFF BAKER, RONALD

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DATA CONVERSIONS INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS FOR CONSTRUCTIVE DISCHARGE. FURTHERMORE, WHETHER A REASONABLE PERSON IN THE EMPLOYEE'S SHOES AND FACED WITH THE ALLEGED INTOLERABLE WORKING CONDITIONS WOULD HAVE FELT COMPELLED TO RESIGN IS A QUESTION OF FACT NOT PROPERLY DECIDED ON DEMURRER. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS ARE NOT BARRED BY WORKER'S COMPENSATION. HERE, PLAINTIFF'S INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIMS ARE PREMISED UPON DEFENDANTS' VIOLATION OF A FUNDAMENTAL PUBLIC POLICY, AND SUCH MISCONDUCT LIES OUTSIDE OF THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY PROVISIONS OF THE LABOR CODE. LEIBERT V. TRANSWORLD SYSTEMS, INC. (1995), 32 CAL.APP.4TH 1693, 1706-1707; ACCARDI V. SUPERIOR COURT (1993), 17 CAL.APP.4TH 341, 352-353. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-13-2014 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-27-2013 Set For Trial On Feb-10-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Karen Tynan, Esq. of the Law Office of Karen Tynan, Tel. 707.395.0062, for defendant and moving party;
Elisa J. Stewart, Esq. of Stewart Musell, Tel. 415.274.0700, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DATA CONVERSIONS INC.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts for constructive discharge. Furthermore, whether a reasonable person in the employee's shoes and faced with the alleged intolerable working conditions would have felt compelled to resign is a question of fact not properly decided on demurrer. Plaintiff's claims for intentional infliction of emotional distress and negligent infliction of emotional distress are not barred by worker's compensation. Here, Plaintiff's intentional infliction of emotional distress and negligent infliction of emotional distress claims are premised upon Defendants' violation of a fundamental public policy, and such misconduct lies outside of the exclusive remedy provisions of the Labor Code. Leibert v. Transworld Systems, Inc. (1995), 32 Cal.App.4th 1693, 1706-1707; Accardi v. Superior Court (1993), 17 Cal.App.4th 341, 352-353. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF RONALD BAKER's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint is granted. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment and allowing plaintiff to file the pleading does not prejudice the defendants. Plaintiff shall immediately file the second amended complaint. After the second amended complaint is filed, the Court will take the motion to quash set for March 28, 2013 and Demurrer set for April 2, 2013 off calendar as moot. Defendants may file new responses to the second amended complaint after it is served. The Court signed the order that was emailed to the court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
